heroText: Brasilia is a capital city in Brazil, connecting local landmarks, event context, transport access and seasonal reasons to plan a visit.

highlights.name: Brasilia
highlights.text: Brasilia is a capital city in Brazil, connecting local landmarks, event context, transport access and seasonal reasons to plan a visit.

history.text: Stating that "The Union shall own, in the central plateau of the Republic, an area of 14,400 square kilometers, which will be demarcated in due course to establish the future federal capital." The government.
The idea of relocating Brazil's capital city was conceived in 1827 by José Bonifácio, an advisor to Emperor Pedro I.
According to a legend, Italian saint Don Bosco in 1883 had a dream in which he described a futuristic city that roughly fitted Brasília's location.
After the Proclamation of the Republic, the Third Article of the new Constitution from 1891 stated that the capital should be moved from Rio de Janeiro to a place close to the country's center.
Already in 1892, the astronomer Louis Cruls, in the service of the Brazilian government, had investigated the site for the future capital.

history.label: 20th century
history.text: Though automobiles were invented prior to the 20th century, mass production of vehicles in the early 20th made them widely available; thus, they became a symbol of modernity.

In 1922 to celebrate the 100th anniversary of Brazil's independence, President Epitácio Pessoa ordered the construction of an obelisk in the Cruls Quadrilateral, known as the Foundation Stone of Brasília, to mark the location.
Upon taking office in January 1956, in fulfilment of his campaign pledge, he initiated the planning and construction of the new capital.

history.label: Modern city
history.text: Brasilia's geographically central location fostered a more regionally neutral federal capital.

history.label: Civic role
history.text: He presented a plan to the General Assembly of Brazil for a new city called Brasília, with the idea of moving the capital westward from the heavily populated southeastern corridor.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: In Brasília today, many references to Bosco, who founded the Salesian order, are found throughout the city and one church parish in the city bears his name.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: But one can not ignore the bus transportation system in the city.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: Additionally, the metro system in Brasília was mainly built for inhabitants of satellite cities.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: However, Brasília was constructed soon after the end of World War II.
